original_description: Upload local images to a GitHub PR and embed them in the description or comments. Use when asked to "attach screenshots to PR", "add images to PR", "upload test results to PR", "embed screenshots in PR description", "add before/after images to PR", "attach UI screenshots", "show test results in PR", "add visual evidence to PR", or any request involving images and PRs. Always use this skill when the user wants to visually document changes in a pull request, even if they don't use the word "upload" — phrases like "put the screenshot in the PR" or "show the image in the PR" should trigger this skill.
best_description: Upload local images to a GitHub PR and embed them in the description or comments. Use when asked to "attach screenshots to PR", "add images to PR", "upload test results to PR", "embed screenshots in PR description", "add before/after images to PR", "attach UI screenshots", "show test results in PR", "add visual evidence to PR", or any request involving images and PRs. Always use this skill when the user wants to visually document changes in a pull request, even if they don't use the word "upload" — phrases like "put the screenshot in the PR" or "show the image in the PR" should trigger this skill.
final_description: Use this skill to put a local image file into an existing GitHub pull request — either by editing the PR description/body or by posting it as a PR comment. The signature pattern is: a message that mentions BOTH a local image (a file path, screenshot, screen recording, CleanShot capture, before/after picture, UI capture, CI failure image, or .png/.jpg/.gif/.webp file) AND a pull request (#1234, a PR URL, "the PR for branch X", "the open PR", "the PR I just opened"). The verb does not matter — upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, include, show, document, "can this go on", or any phrasing of "make this image appear in that PR" all qualify. Also use when the user wants to visually document tests, repros, UI states, bug evidence, or CI failures inside an existing PR. Do NOT use for: capturing or taking a new screenshot, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images inside repo files like README.md.

history.description: Upload local images to a GitHub PR and embed them in the description or comments. Use when asked to "attach screenshots to PR", "add images to PR", "upload test results to PR", "embed screenshots in PR description", "add before/after images to PR", "attach UI screenshots", "show test results in PR", "add visual evidence to PR", or any request involving images and PRs. Always use this skill when the user wants to visually document changes in a pull request, even if they don't use the word "upload" — phrases like "put the screenshot in the PR" or "show the image in the PR" should trigger this skill.

history.description: Use this skill whenever a user wants a local image file (screenshot, diagram, screen recording frame, CleanShot capture, test result image, before/after picture, etc.) to end up inside a GitHub pull request — either embedded in the PR description or posted as a PR comment. Trigger on ANY pairing of (a) a local image, identified by file path, filename, "screenshot", "image", "screen recording", "cleanshot", or by location like Desktop/Downloads/tmp, with (b) a GitHub PR identified by number (#1234), URL, branch name ("the PR for branch X"), or phrases like "the open PR" or "the PR I just opened". Verbs vary widely: upload, attach, embed, add, put, place, drop, slap, stick, include, show, display, document, post. Also trigger when the user wants to visually document changes, CI failures, UI states, or test runs in a PR. Do NOT trigger for: capturing new screenshots, editing/resizing images, pushing branches, opening new PRs without existing images, or referencing images inside repo files (README, markdown).

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants an existing local image file to appear inside a GitHub pull request — embedded in the PR body or posted as a PR comment. Trigger whenever a message references both: an image (a file path ending in .png/.jpg/.gif/.webp, or words like screenshot, image, screen recording, CleanShot, before/after, UI capture, CI failure screenshot) AND a pull request (PR number like #1234, PR URL, "the PR for branch X", "the open PR", "the PR I just opened"). Trigger regardless of verb — upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto". Also trigger for visually documenting test runs, bug repros, UI states, or CI failures in a PR. Do NOT trigger for: capturing/taking new screenshots, resizing or editing images, opening a new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images inside repo files like README or markdown docs.

history.description: Use this skill whenever a user has a local image file (screenshot, screen recording, CleanShot capture, before/after, UI capture, CI failure image, or any .png/.jpg/.gif/.webp file) AND wants it to land in a GitHub pull request — whether in the PR body/description or as a PR comment. The PR may be referenced by number (#1234), URL, branch name ("the PR for X branch"), or as "the open PR" / "the PR I just opened". Trigger on any verb expressing placement: upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, display, document, "can this go on", "get this onto", "make this appear in". Trigger for visually documenting tests, repros, UI states, bug evidence, or CI failures inside an existing PR. Do NOT trigger for: capturing/taking new screenshots, editing/resizing images, opening a new PR from scratch, pushing branches, or referencing images inside repo files like README.md or other markdown docs.
